{"step_by_step_thinking": "Macrocephaly is an enlarged head size, which can be a consequence of fetal alcohol syndrome. Holoprosencephaly is a condition where the forebrain fails to divide properly, and it is listed as a correlation with mutations in the sonic hedgehog signaling pathway, Trisomy 13, and fetal alcohol syndrome. Microcephaly is characterized by a smaller than normal head size, which can also be a result of fetal alcohol syndrome. Thinning of the corpus callosum is a known finding in fetal alcohol syndrome. Therefore, the only option that is not a sequela of fetal alcohol syndrome is B: Holoprosencephaly.", "answer_choice": "B"}
